title: Sugarcane Bud Cutter — Rotary Blade with Node Sensor
design_question: How to automatically cut sugarcane stalks at inter-node positions preserving bud viability?
selected_approach: Conveyor belt (0.15 m/s) feeds stalks through a V-groove guide. TCR5000 optical reflectance sensor detects node (20% reflectance change). Arduino triggers 300 mm TCT circular saw blade (3000 RPM, 750 W) at correct inter-node position. Pneumatic clamp holds stalk during cut. Cut sett length 250–300 mm (2 buds per sett).
rationale: Optical node detection enables automatic positioning without manual measurement. TCT blade handles repeated cuts through fibrous 40 mm stalks without dulling. Conveyor-based system achieves 600 setts/hour vs 100/hour manual.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Sugarcane Processing — Node Cutting and Bud Viability' gives cutting force and germination rate data.
supporting_fact_titles: Sugarcane Processing — Node Cutting and Bud Viability
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
